steps.title: Identify which tribe's data the blank requires
steps.content: The passage names a specific tribe—Los Coyotes Band of Cahuilla and Cupeño Indians in Southern California—as the example. The blank must be filled with this tribe's actual area from the table.

steps.title: Look up the tribe in the table and confirm it supports the claim
steps.content:
The table shows Los Coyotes Band of Cahuilla and Cupeño Indians at 39.21 square miles.

This figure is less than 50, directly supporting the statement that most tribal nations are under 50 square miles.

The other choices are areas from different tribal nations in the table—La Jolla Band (13.50), Pauma Band (9.36), and Agua Caliente Band (53.68)—and do not represent Los Coyotes Band.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
